title: Sharing Experiences
learningGoal: Speak fluently to answer questions about experiences using the present perfect tense and facts using the passive voice.
instructions: 外国人の友人と経験について話しています。質問に対して、「Yes, I have...」や「No, I have never...」を使って答えてください。マイクに向かって英語で話しましょう。(You are sharing experiences with a foreign friend. Answer their questions using the present perfect and passive voice.)
speakingPrompt: Answer questions about your experiences using the present perfect tense.
scenario: A friend is asking you about your life in Japan.

subTasks.prompt: Your friend asks: 'Have you ever been to Kyoto?' Answer: 'Yes, I have been to Kyoto.'

sampleResponse.targetText: Yes, I have been to Kyoto.
sampleResponse.transliteration: イエス、アイ ハヴ ビーン トゥ キョウト。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: はい、京都に行ったことがあります。

subTasks.prompt: Your friend asks: 'Have you ever eaten natto?' Answer: 'No, I have never eaten natto.'

sampleResponse.targetText: No, I have never eaten natto.
sampleResponse.transliteration: ノー、アイ ハヴ ネヴァー イートン ナットウ。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: いいえ、私は一度も納豆を食べたことがありません。

subTasks.prompt: Your friend asks: 'Was this camera made in Japan?' Answer: 'Yes, it was made in Japan.'

sampleResponse.targetText: Yes, it was made in Japan.
sampleResponse.transliteration: イエス、イット ワズ メイド イン ジャパン。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: はい、それは日本で作られました（日本製です）。

scenarioContext.targetText: We are talking about Japan. Tell me about your experiences!
scenarioContext.transliteration: ウィー アー トーキング アバウト ジャパン。テル ミー アバウト ユア エクスペリエンシズ！
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: 日本について話しているね。あなたの経験を教えて！

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, I have been to Kyoto.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: イエス、アイ ハヴ ビーン トゥ キョウト。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: はい、京都に行ったことがあります。

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: No, I have never eaten natto.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ノー、アイ ハヴ ネヴァー イートン ナットウ。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: いいえ、私は一度も納豆を食べたことがありません。

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, it was made in Japan.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: イエス、イット ワズ メイド イン ジャパン。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: はい、それは日本で作られました（日本製です）。

expectedSpeakingPoints: Using 'have been to' for travel experience.
Using 'have never eaten' for food experience.
Using passive voice 'was made in' for a fact.
acceptableParaphrases: Yes, I've been to Kyoto. No, I've never eaten natto. Yes, it was made in Japan.

criteria.name: Sentence Structure
criteria.description: Must use 'I have' for experience and 'It was' for passive voice.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Yes, I have been to Kyoto. No, I have never eaten natto. Yes, it was made in Japan.

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent speaking! You confidently answered using the correct grammar structures.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Make sure you use the past participles (been, eaten, made) and not the basic verbs (go, eat, make).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember to use 'have never' to emphasize that you haven't done something in your whole life.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Yes, I have been to Kyoto. No, I have never eaten natto. Yes, it was made in Japan.

hintText: Use 'I have never...' to say you haven't done it.
commonMistakes: Saying 'I have gone to Kyoto' instead of 'I have been to Kyoto'.
